question: To manually set up a custom connector project (without using the GitHub template repository), which Maven dependency does the Connector SDK Setup section instruct you to include?

a.text: The SDK Setup section lists exactly this dependency: groupId `io.camunda.connector`, artifactId `connector-core`, version `${version.connectors}`. The Gradle equivalent uses the same coordinates.

b.text: There is no `connector-sdk` artifactId published under `io.camunda`. The SDK ships as `io.camunda.connector:connector-core`.

c.text: `zeebe-client-java` is the Zeebe Java client library, not the Connector SDK; the SDK Setup section does not include it.

d.text: `connector-runtime` belongs to the runtime application that hosts connectors at execution time, not the SDK dependency you add while building a connector.

explanation: The Connector SDK's Setup section instructs developers to add `io.camunda.connector:connector-core:${version.connectors}` as a Maven dependency, or the equivalent Gradle implementation entry. This artifact provides the SDK's `OutboundConnectorFunction` and `InboundConnectorExecutable` interfaces (and supporting types) that you implement when writing a custom connector.
